studies.title: Evaluation of "Invirto aftercare" for anxiety disorders: a pilot study.
studies.brief_summary: The aim of the planned study is to investigate feasibility, acceptance as well as safety and efficacy of an online therapy for the aftercare of a psychotherapeutic treatment ("Invirto Aftercare"). A good feasibility and acceptance by the affected person as well as a stable reduction of anxiety symptoms including low relapse rates over the study period is expected. Within the scope of the study, 70 patients with diagnosed anxiety disorders and previous psychotherapy will be recruited and interviewed at three points in time using self-assessment questionnaires (online survey).

classification.reason: The study does not explicitly mention randomization, control groups, or interventional design. It appears to be an observational study using self-assessment questionnaires at multiple time points, which aligns with RWE characteristics. The lack of clear RCT indicators like 'randomized' or 'controlled' leads to a conservative classification as RWE.
